title: How to understand your BackShip invoices
bodyHtml:
This article will help you understand your BackShip invoices. If you want to understand your general invoices, check out this article

Your general invoices are uploaded to your Back Office on the 6th, 16th, and 26th day of every month. Depending on the invoice, most will include 7 sections: 

  1. Summary of the invoice for that period
  2. Number of orders confirmed during that period
  3. Number of orders refunded during that period
  4. Total support service for each order during that period
  5. Number of goodwill gestures for that period
  6. Number of BackShip orders during that period
  7. Total adjustments for previous BackShip invoices


This article focuses on sections 6 and 7. 

You’re billed based on an estimation of BackShip transportation fees. We use our in-house data to best calculate this price.  The shipping fees appear in the 6th section of your bill (under the “DETAILS DES BACKSHIP SCANNÉS AVANT LE DD/MM/YYY” headline), just as shown in the image below:



It’s possible that the estimated price will not match the final price, and Back Market will adjust the price by crediting or debiting the difference on your next invoice. You’ll find the adjustments in section 7 of your invoice, under the banner “DÉTAILS DES AVOIRS SUR COMMISSIONS BACK MARKET” or “DÉTAILS DES RÉGULARISATIONS DE FRAIS DE SERVICE BACKSHIP.” 





The credit or debit will appear on a future invoice as the estimated price that was billed to you has to be compared to the actual cost of shipping. It usually appears the month after shipping. The adjustment can be found on the invoice with the order number. See the timeline below:

 

BackShip Billing Update


In order to further improve your BackShip experience, we’re changing our billing process to be more simple, transparent, and easy to manage. As mentioned in this article, you’re billed when you ship your package via BackShip. If the charges are over- or underestimated, an adjustment is made the following month. 

Starting in October, you’ll be charged the exact amount only after the exact price of your shipment is known. You’ll be charged on a monthly basis after all shipping is confirmed. Beginning in September, these changes will be piloted with DHL Express and in the future, other shipping providers will be brought onto the new invoicing process.

If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to reach out to your Back Market point of contact.
 

Frequently Asked Questions


How does Back Market measure and charge per package?

Packages are billed based on the shipping provider’s measurements
Most shipping providers use the highest measurement between the weight and the volumetric weight
 

How do I reconcile estimated shipping costs with the potential adjustments?

You can search your orders by order number. The order amount, the shipping cost in section 6, and any potential adjustment in section 7 will all show the order number.









Tip: Use the CTRL+F shortcut to reconcile the different credits and debits in your bill associated to the same order number.

Because the adjustment is only calculated after the first invoice, the shipping charge and its adjustment will appear on different invoices (usually 2 or 3 10-day periods or invoices apart). 


What can I expect to be adjusted?

The number of items to be adjusted depends on the seller and the type of item. Larger items often have greater variations in weight measurement. However, we’re consistently working on reducing adjustments.


How many adjustments per order should I expect?

There’s 1 adjustment per order at the most. 

In some exceptional cases, you may see a second adjustment if you have claimed the price of a package after it had been adjusted and Back Market still needs to reconcile the price due to a shipping provider bill error. 


What can I do to reduce my adjustment rate?

We recommend that you keep in mind that the packages sent are billed based on the weight measurement from the shipping providers.

You should pay attention to both the actual weight and the volumetric weight to be consistently billed the same amount and avoid adjustments. This is particularly true for large items. 

We ask that you avoid calling the shipping provider for unnecessary collections as these may be also billed. 


Where can I see the weight used to charge me for my package?

Shipping providers don’t share the weight of each package, meaning that Back Market isn’t able to show it on your BackShip invoices. But we’re working on getting this data to consistently show it on invoices.
